'''Christopher Thorman''' (born 26 September 1980) is an English professional [[rugby league]] coach who is an assistant coach at the [[York Knights]] in the [[Super League]], the former head coach of the [[Newcastle Thunder]] and a former professional rugby league footballer. 

An [[England national rugby league team|England international]] [[Goal (sport)#Rugby football|goal]]-kicking {{rlp|so}}, he previously played in the [[Super League]] for [[Hull FC]], [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] and the [[Sheffield Eagles]], as well as in the [[National Rugby League]] for the [[Parramatta Eels]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> Following the sacking of [[Rick Stone]] as coach of the [[Huddersfield Giants]],<ref name="Coach Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> he was announced as interim head coach.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/43555065|title=Rick Stone: Huddersfield Giants sack coach after poor start to season|date=2018-03-27|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2018-03-27|language=en-GB}}</ref>

==Background==
Born 26 September 1980 in [[Wallsend]], [[North Tyneside]], Thorman went to Western First School, Western Middle School and then onto Burnside Community High School (now known as [[Burnside Business and Enterprise College]]), where he was friends with former [[England National Football Team|England football]] international [[Michael Carrick]]. His first rugby club was Newcastle Eagles (now known as [[Wallsend Eagles]]) where he played with his younger brothers, Paul and Neil, as a youngster under the watchful eye of Simon Wilkinson, the club's head coach. He was discovered by a scout and sent to regional camps and other rugby league trials. Playing for [North East U16] more and more scouts discovered the potential the young man had. At 16, he made his professional début playing for the Sheffield Eagles.

==Playing career==
Thorman scored the fastest hat-trick of tries scored from the start of a match in 6 min 54 sec while playing for Huddersfield Giants against Doncaster Dragons in the semi-final of the Buddies National League Cup at Doncaster, South Yorkshire, on 19 May 2002.<ref name="Fastest rugby league hat-trick">{{cite web|url=http://www.guinnessworldrecords.com/Search/Details/Fastestrugby-league-hat-trick/58889.htm|title=Fastest rugby league hat-trick|publisher=guinnessworldrecords.com|date=31 December 2010|accessdate=2010-01-01}}</ref>

In 2004, Thorman made a highly anticipated move to [[NRL]] side the [[Parramatta Eels]].  Thorman struggled with form and only made 11 appearances for the club before leaving Australia and signing with The [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2004/jul/28/rugbyleague.huddersfieldgiants|title = Rugby League: Huddersfield seal another transfer coup as Thorman returns| newspaper=The Guardian |date = 28 July 2004 | last1=Wilson | first1=Andy }}</ref>

His representative honours include 1 game for Yorkshire, 1 game for England "A" and 2 games as England captain. Thorman captained Huddersfield in the [[2006 Challenge Cup]] Final at stand-off half back against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] but the Giants lost 12–42. In June 2007 Thorman was called up to the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] squad for the [[test match (rugby league)|Test match]] against [[France national rugby league team|France]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/6744909.stm|title=Eight new faces in Lions squad|publisher=BBC|date=12 June 2007|access-date=2007-06-12}}</ref> however missed the match with an injury to his thigh.
Thorman signed a 1-year deal for Hull in the 2009 season but after a disappointing season for Hull he was sold to [[York City Knights]]. In March 2011, Thorman set a record for the highest individual score in the Challenge Cup by scoring 56 points as York won 132–0 over Northumbria University.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/8893803.records-tumble-as-york-city-knights-trounce-students-132-0/ |title=Records tumble as York City Knights trounce students 132-0 |newspaper=The Press |date=7 March 2011 |access-date=24 December 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/31094/ |title=Challenge Cup: First Round wrap-up |publisher=Rugby-League.com |date=28 January 2017 |access-date=24 December 2023}}</ref>

==Coaching career==
In June 2012, Thorman announced to be joining Super League's Huddersfield Giants as an assistant coach at the end of the season.

On 3 May 2019, Thorman was appointed head coach at [[Workington Town]], following the departure of former boss [[Leon Pryce]]. His move followed his resignation from his first management job at [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/workington-appoint-chris-thorman-as-new-coach/|title=Workington appoint Chris Thorman as new coach|publisher=guinnessworldrecords.com|date=3 May 2019|accessdate=4 May 2019}}</ref>

On 21 June 2025 it was reported that [[Graham Steadman]] had taken over as interim head coach after Chris left to take up a new role as assistant coach at [[York Knights]], under [[Mark Applegarth]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=21 Jun 2025|accessdate=22 Jun 2025|author=James Gordon|title=Former Great Britain star takes charge of Newcastle Thunder after Chris Thorman exit|url=https://www.totalrl.com/former-great-britain-star-takes-charge-of-newcastle-thunder-after-chris-thorman-exit/}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=23 Jun 2025|author=Doug Thomson|title=Mark Applegarth grateful for new assistant at York Knights|url=https://www.totalrl.com/mark-applegarth-grateful-for-new-assistant-at-york-knights/}}</ref>
